title: Asking about medicine at a pharmacy
description: Describe symptoms and ask a pharmacist for something to help
context: Watsons and Manning's are the two big pharmacy chains in HK. Pharmacists here are trained and happy to recommend over-the-counter medicines — you don't always need a doctor for minor ailments. Describe your symptoms clearly.
cultural_note: The invisible framework behind most self-medication in Hong Kong is 熱氣 (jit6 hei3, "hot energy"): fried food, alcohol, chillies, and late nights are understood to cause internal heat that shows up as sore throats, mouth ulcers, and restlessness. The antidote is 涼 (leong4, "cooling") food and drink, chrysanthemum tea, winter melon juice, or the bitter herbal teas sold from open-front shops on almost every street.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 唔該，我喉嚨痛，有冇藥可以食？
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, ngo5 hau4 lung4 tung3, jau5 mou5 joek6 ho2 ji5 sik6?
lines.english: Excuse me, I have a sore throat. Is there something I can take?

lines.speaker: pharmacist
lines.cjk: 有，呢款喉糖效果好好。係咪有痰？
lines.jyutping: jau5, ni1 fun2 hau4 tong2 haau6 gwo2 hou2 hou2. hai6 mai6 jau5 taam4?
lines.english: Yes, these throat lozenges work well. Do you also have phlegm?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 係，有少少。同埋有啲流鼻水。
lines.jyutping: hai6, jau5 siu2 siu2. tung4 maai4 jau5 di1 lau4 bei6 seoi2.
lines.english: Yes, a little. And I have a bit of a runny nose too.

lines.speaker: pharmacist
lines.cjk: 噉我建議你買呢隻傷風藥，朝早食兩粒，夜晚食兩粒。
lines.jyutping: gam2 ngo5 gin3 ji5 nei5 maai5 ni1 zek3 soeng1 fung1 joek6, ziu1 zou2 sik6 loeng5 nap1, je6 maan5 sik6 loeng5 nap1.
lines.english: In that case I'd suggest this cold medicine. Two tablets in the morning, two at night.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: sik6 jyun4 jau5 mou5 fu3 zok3 jung6?

lines.speaker: pharmacist
lines.cjk: 有啲人食完會眼瞓，最好唔好揸車。
lines.jyutping: jau5 di1 jan4 sik6 jyun4 wui5 ngaan5 fan3, zeoi3 hou2 m4 hou2 zaa1 ce1.
lines.english: Some people feel drowsy after taking it. Best not to drive.
